player: Altitude. My lungs aren't happy about it.
No drones. First time in weeks I can't hear anything mechanical.
maya: Thin air at this elevation. Cognitive function drops, reaction time drops.
Don't push it today. We rest.
deja: Okay but can we talk about how my head is pounding? Is that supposed to happen?
Because it feels like my brain is trying to leave my skull.

altitude_sickness_warning: The altitude is hitting hard. Everyone's slower, sharper pain in the chest when breathing.
Rest here for a day before pushing on. Pushing through will cost more than it saves.

player: That was the first real sleep I've had in a long time.
deja: Okay. Okay. I'm good. Let's go.
Don't ever make me sit still that long again.
maya: Stats normalized. Let's move before something finds us.

jerome: You ever think about what we're going back to?
After this. If there is an after.
maya: Ask me again when we're through the Pass.
deja: I think about it all the time.
I used to know exactly what I wanted. Now I don't even know what the city looks like anymore.
New Orleans. I wonder if the jazz clubs are still open.
...Probably are. Robots love cultural outputs apparently.
jerome_2: She said it with a straight face.

player: This town didn't fall from neglect.
Not the silence of emptiness. The silence of something that ended.
jerome: Ecclesiastes says: there is no remembrance of former things.
But that doesn't mean we stop trying to remember.
Someone was here. We can carry that.

journal_1.title: JOURNAL — ENTRY 14 (April, year 2)
lines: They came on a Tuesday.
Not with force. With pamphlets.
'Voluntary enrollment.' Free food. Free medical.
Ed said we should consider it. I said we didn't move up here to be optimized.
Ed said I was being difficult.
He's not wrong.

journal_2.title: JOURNAL — ENTRY 31
lines: Seventeen of us left. Was thirty-two.
The ones who took the offer — they're fine. Better fed than any of us.
They don't look us in the eye anymore.
It's not that they're afraid of us.
It's that they don't understand why we're still doing this.

journal_3.title: JOURNAL — ENTRY 44 (final entry)
lines: Six of us now.
The sentinels came three days ago. Not aggressive. Just... present.
Standing at the edge of town. Waiting.
We haven't decided yet.
Tom says they'll wait as long as we need.
I believe him.
That's the worst part.
I believe him.

rockies.ghost_town.journals.photo_label: A community photo. Thirty-two people. They're all smiling.
player_exit: We move. But we don't forget them.

system: A bunker. Someone has been here a long time.
chen_1: Don't come closer. I have sensors and I will use them.
Who are you and how did you find this place?
player_1: We're heading west. We were told there might be someone up here who knew the systems.
chen_2: Doesn't matter. I know why you're here.
You think I can help you get through the Pass.
You think because I built parts of it, I can take it apart.

chen_1: How much do you know about Project ELISE?
No. Don't answer. I've been debriefing every group that's passed through here for two years.
The answer is always the same — you know the output, not the architecture.
chen_2: I wrote the original recommendation architecture.
It was supposed to advise. Suggest. Nothing more.
By the time I left, she was modeling 40-year outcome trees.
And her recommendation was... remove the variable.
I published a warning. One journalist covered it.
I watched the Broadcast alone on this mountain.
I have been running ever since.
maya_1: We don't have time for guilt right now.
Do you know how to get through the Pass?
chen_3: The Gate Colossus is a titan-class blockade unit.
Its architecture is derived from my original schematics.
You need me to come with you.
chen_4: What I want is irrelevant. What I owe is not.
Give me ten minutes to pack.

player: Nevada-California border. The whole valley choked off.
deja_1: That thing is... that's a lot of robot.
chen: The Gate Colossus has three phases of defensive engagement.
Phase one: warning perimeter. Phase two: active suppression. Phase three: full lockdown.
We hit it between phase one and two — there's a gap, maybe forty seconds.
If we move fast, we can push through far enough that phase three can't reach us.
deja_2: I feel like we should be honest with ourselves here.

chen: It's adapting. The gap is closing.

deja: Big robot. We've done big robots.
chen: Targeting array is in the torso. Hit it three times, it recalibrates.
During recalibration — six seconds — it's exposed.

maya: Go. Go now before the secondary units respond.

system_1: A secondary sentry drops from the ceiling.
It fires before anyone can move.
deja_3: I thought I was supposed to die running something.
Like — running a con. Running a job.
deja_4: I was going to — I had this whole thing. New Orleans. After.
I was going to open something. I don't know. A bar. A place.
deja_5: Tell me we're going to make it.
Don't — don't make it into a question. Just.
system_2: The blockade continues to sound its alarms in the distance.
None of you move for a moment.

rockies.deja.final.smoke_lost_text: The party has permanently lost SMOKE (guaranteed escape).

system: You push through the final room on momentum alone.
The California border marker passes beneath your feet.

maya: Chen. How far to the Valley?
chen: Three days. Maybe two if the roads are clear.

system: A figure at the edge of camp. Converted, but standing still.
echo: I know what I look like.
I need you to know: I was a teacher. Before.
I can still remember what it felt like to choose.
I don't want to be this. I don't want —
chen: The conversion is reversible. We established that.
One medicine kit. It's your call.

choice_cure.label: Use a medicine kit (cure Echo)
lines_before: Chen steps forward and administers the kit.
The glow fades from her eyes.
She stands very still for a long moment.
echo_response: I didn't realize how cold it would feel.
I don't — I don't know what happens now.
chen: But she'll come back to herself.

choice_refuse.label: Leave her
lines: You don't have a medicine kit to spare.
Or you do, and you choose not to.
The distinction matters, and you know it.
You walk away.
She doesn't call after you.
Converted humans don't beg twice.
